emails.subject: Leave Request — Recurring Migraines
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I'm Rosa Gutierrez (E1051, Production). I get severe migraines that sometimes keep me from working. My doctor says they happen about 1-2 times a month and can last a full day. I would like to request intermittent leave for these episodes.

I have been with Ridgeline since June 2019.

Please let me know what I need to do.

Thank you,
Rosa Gutierrez
Production Worker — Fort Collins Campus

emails.subject: Re: Leave Request — Recurring Migraines [Case LC-2026-0089]
emails.body_text:
Hi Rosa,

Thank you for reaching out. I have received your request for intermittent leave for recurring migraines and have opened Case LC-2026-0089.

FMLA ELIGIBILITY: Based on your hire date of June 3, 2019, and your hours on file, you meet all three FMLA eligibility criteria. You are eligible for up to 480 hours of FMLA leave in the rolling 12-month period.

FAMLI: You may also be eligible for Colorado FAMLI wage replacement for the same qualifying reason. I have included information about FAMLI below.

NEXT STEP — MEDICAL CERTIFICATION: Please have your healthcare provider complete the attached WH-380-E (Employee's Serious Health Condition) form and return it to me within 15 calendar days (by February 18, 2026).

I have also notified your manager, Marcus Evans, that you have a leave case open. He has not been told the nature of your condition.

Please do not hesitate to reply to this email if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Re: Leave Request — Recurring Migraines [Case LC-2026-0089]
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Please find my completed medical certification attached.

Thank you,
Rosa

emails.subject: Re: Leave Request — Recurring Migraines [Case LC-2026-0089] — Designation Notice
emails.body_text:
Hi Rosa,

I have received your completed medical certification. Based on the information provided, your leave is designated as FMLA-qualifying under Case LC-2026-0089.

DESIGNATION DETAILS:
• Leave Type: Intermittent FMLA + concurrent Colorado FAMLI
• Qualifying Reason: Employee's own serious health condition (recurring migraines)
• Certified Frequency: Up to 2 episodes per month, 1 day per episode
• Certification Period: Through August 1, 2026
• FMLA Balance: 480 hours available in the rolling 12-month period

HOW TO USE YOUR LEAVE: When a migraine prevents you from working, please notify your manager and reply to this email so I can log the absence. You do not need to call in separately to HR — notifying your manager is sufficient.

FAMLI: CDLE has been notified and will send you information about your wage replacement claim separately.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: FAMLI Employer Response Required — Claim FAMLI-2026-00041 — Rosa Gutierrez
emails.body_text:
COLORADO DEPARTMENT OF LABOR AND EMPLOYMENT
Family and Medical Leave Insurance Program

Claim Notice: FAMLI-2026-00041
Employee: Rosa Gutierrez
Employer: Ridgeline Gear Co.
Claim Start Date: February 2, 2026

An employee at your organization has filed a FAMLI claim. You have 5 business days from the date of this notice to submit your employer response. Failure to respond by February 16, 2026 will result in your right to contest being forfeited.

Please log in to your CDLE employer portal to submit your response.

Colorado Department of Labor and Employment
FAMLI Division | claims@famli.colorado.gov

emails.subject: Re: LC-2026-0089 — Rosa Gutierrez — March Absence
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Just a heads-up — Marcus Evans let me know Rosa used one intermittent FMLA day on March 4. Please log it in leave_tracker.xlsx (case LC-2026-0089) and update the running balance. Running total should now be 3 days / 24 hours used.

Thanks,
Renata

emails.subject: Leave Request [Case LC-2026-0046]
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I need to take some time off for a family medical situation. My spouse is recovering from surgery and needs care at home. I expect to be out from March 22 through approximately April 11.

Please let me know what paperwork I need.

Thank you,
Indira Carroll

emails.subject: Bereavement Leave Request [Case LC-2026-0042]
emails.body_text:
Good morning,

My father passed away yesterday. I need to take bereavement leave starting today. I expect to be out through April 17. My manager is aware.

Penelope Barnes

emails.subject: Re: Bereavement Leave Request [Case LC-2026-0042]
emails.body_text:
Hi Penelope,

I am very sorry for your loss.

I have opened Case LC-2026-0042 for your bereavement leave. Ridgeline's policy provides 3 days of paid bereavement leave for an immediate family member. Your manager has been notified of your absence dates.

Please provide documentation (such as an obituary or funeral program) within 30 calendar days of the date of loss.

Please take the time you need. I am here if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Open Cases Check-In — Week of April 6
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Quick end-of-week check-in on open cases:

• LC-2026-0046 (Indira Carroll, FAMLI): Expected return April 11 — confirm she returns Monday and close the case if so.
• LC-2026-0089 (Rosa Gutierrez, FMLA/FAMLI intermittent): No new absences reported this week — correct?
• LC-2026-0042 (Penelope Barnes, Bereavement): Still open through April 17.
• LC-2026-0009 (Ivan Anderson, FAMLI): On track, no issues reported.

Have a good weekend.

Renata

emails.subject: Question about PTO + FAMLI, and time off for my mom
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I heard from a Gabe (who was told by Teresa Vang) that I might be able to use my PTO on top of my FAMLI pay so I don't lose as much money when I take my intermittent medical leaves. Is that true?

Also, my mom passed away last night. She lived in Mexico and the funeral will be there. My manager already knows. I need to take a full week off starting April 20th.

Rosa Gutierrez (E1051)
Assistant Production Lead

emails.subject: Jury Duty — Starting April 27
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I received a jury summons for Larimer County District Court. I'm scheduled to report on May 27 and they said it could last up to two weeks. I've let my manager Marcus Evans know.

I've attached a copy of the summons. Let me know what else you need from me.

Thanks,
Kevin Price
Fulfillment Specialist — Operations
